summary.intent: Implement task #267: the framework-agnostic TreeView core component (expand/collapse, selection, keyboard nav, typeahead, ARIA wiring, DnD reorder variant) in the ars-ui Rust workspace, syncing the spec as the authoritative contract.
summary.outcome: Full TreeView agnostic core implemented (mod.rs ~1700 lines, 126 unit tests, 26 snapshots, spec-conformance, proptest), PR #695 opened and pushed through 5 rounds of Codex review (33 total findings, all addressed with TDD regression tests and spec sync); awaiting final Codex 👍 and user merge decision.

repo: TreeCollection::nodes() and keys() iterate visible_indices only; collapsed subtrees are invisible. Added all_nodes()/all_keys() accessors to reach every node regardless of expansion state — required for ExpandAll correctness.

Key does not implement Borrow<str>; Key::int(5) and Key::from("5") produce different variants that never compare equal in BTreeSet<Key>. The spec's String-based node identity was wrong — Key must be used throughout.
EffectName has a blanket impl in ars-core; manually implementing it on a new Effect type causes a conflict. Never impl EffectName manually.
selection::Set is #[non_exhaustive]; match arms against it require a wildcard catch-all or compilation fails.
selection::State::select already rejects disabled keys (selection.rs ~line 200), but disabled_keys must be seeded at init from TreeItem.disabled flags or disabled nodes remain silently selectable.
DisabledBehavior::FocusOnly allows typeahead to reach disabled nodes (per WAI-ARIA spec) while still blocking selection via State::select — the correct semantic, distinct from DisabledBehavior::Skip.

The shared ars_collections::typeahead::State uses collection.nodes() for visibility; since nodes() is visible-only, process_typeahead must call reconciled_items() (clone ctx.items, apply live ctx.expanded via set_expanded) to search collapsed subtrees correctly.

DraggableCollection::selection(&self) -> &selection::State is a required method; TreeCollection cannot implement it because selection lives in the component Context, not the collection. Adapter-level DnD (use_drag/use_drop, trait impls, DOM hit-testing) belongs to tasks #366/#509.
Api structs require a manual impl Debug for Api — the derive cannot be used because Api holds &Context and &Props borrows. Pattern established in accordion/mod.rs.

Navigation tests use a single flat file tests/spec_conformance/navigation.rs (not per-component split); tree_view tests append there for spec-conformance, and src/navigation/tree_view/tests.rs for unit tests.
The pre-commit hook re-stages all tracked modifications after edits, collapsing an intended two-commit split into one commit. Plan for this when splitting feature + policy changes.
.claude/ and .agents/ skill files are hardlinked (same inode); editing one updates both automatically.
cargo xtask spec compile-snippets uses syn::parse_file (syntax-only, no type resolution); Rust code blocks in the spec only need to parse, not type-check. Still catches syntax regressions.
WAI-ARIA tree pattern (APG) specifies no-wrap for ArrowUp/ArrowDown navigation — focus stops at first/last node. This reverses the common wrap assumption; Codex caught it in round 5.

code.finding: visible_keys() sits in the impl<T: CollectionItem> block; the no-bound impl<T> block (lines 134-175) is the correct home for pure-read accessors like all_keys()/all_nodes() that don't need CollectionItem bounds.

State::select already rejects disabled keys, but disabled_keys must be seeded from TreeItem.disabled flags at init. Missing seeding silently made disabled nodes selectable despite the guard being present.

typeahead::State.process_char_with_locale uses collection.nodes() for visible-node iteration. For TreeView, nodes() only returns currently-visible nodes, so a reconciled_items() shim must clone ctx.items and apply ctx.expanded via set_expanded before passing to the typeahead state.

All agnostic DnD primitives (DropPosition, CollectionDropTarget, CollectionDndEvent, DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection) are in ars_collections::dnd. DraggableCollection::selection() requires live selection state, making implementation on bare TreeCollection impossible — this is the core/adapter split boundary.

workflow: Codex review loop: post @codex review, poll reactions (👀 = reviewing, 👍 = approved, no reaction + unresolved threads = findings). Reply inline with gh api pulls/.../comments -F in_reply_to=, resolve via resolveReviewThread GraphQL mutation, then re-trigger with @codex review. Re-apply snapshot-reviewed label on every commit that touches .snap files.

insta stops each test function at its first unaccepted snapshot; multi-snapshot functions only emit one per run. Run cargo insta test --accept --test-runner cargo-test to generate all, then review before accepting.

cargo mutants buffers progress during a run; results land in mutants.out/ incrementally. The mutants.out/ directory is gitignored but the scheduled_tasks.lock file is not — stage files explicitly to avoid committing runtime artifacts.

Mandatory pre-PR mutation gate was removed for agnostic components per user decision; AGENTS.md and the post-implementation-audit skill were updated. Surviving mutants are now triaged at Nightly CI instead.
xci-fast has 10 gates and must be green before any push. spec validate + compile-snippets are included in the gate and catch spec regressions independently of type-checking.
zsh ${!arr[@]} syntax for associative array iteration fails in Bash-mode tool calls; use bash <<'SCRIPT' ... SCRIPT heredoc or plain bash invocation instead.

Coverage analysis via llvm-cov identifies defensive branches that are structurally unreachable (e.g., re-checking is_draggable when items haven't changed); simplifying those dead branches is preferable to writing contrived tests to cover them.

Codex review rounds converge but don't terminate instantly; budget for 4-6 rounds on a complex component. Each round's findings are genuine P2 correctness issues, not nitpicks — the loop is worth running to completion.

friction: zsh associative-array iteration (${!arr[@]}) failed in the reply-to-threads script, requiring a rewrite to use bash <<'SCRIPT' heredoc invocation.

The pre-commit hook re-staged all tracked modifications when attempting a two-commit split (feature + policy change), collapsing both into a single commit. Required amending the commit message post-facto.
Background mutation run was started, then stopped mid-run when the user changed the policy — some CPU time wasted on a gate that was then eliminated.
cargo-mutants contends on the Cargo build lock with other running cargo processes (proptest, clippy); the background poller failed with a lock conflict and had to be retried.
insta emits only one unaccepted snapshot per test function per run, requiring multiple invocations of cargo insta test --accept to generate the full 26-snapshot baseline.
selection::Set being #[non_exhaustive] was only discovered at compile time after writing a multi-arm match — required restructuring the match with a wildcard catch-all.

Five rounds of Codex review (33 findings total) were required to converge, each requiring a full TDD + spec-sync + commit + reply + resolve cycle. The Codex loop is effective but time-intensive for a complex component.
Context window exhausted mid-session, requiring a full session summary and continuation prompt to resume without losing state.
open_items: Codex round 5 commit and push not yet completed at transcript end; round-5 threads not replied to/resolved; @codex review not yet re-triggered for round 6.
Issue #267 not yet closed — must wait for PR #695 to be merged, CI to pass, and Codex to give 👍 before closing.

Adapter tasks #366 (TreeView Leptos adapter) and #509 (TreeView Dioxus adapter) are open and depend on this PR being merged; they own Draggable/DroppableCollection trait impls, DOM hit-testing, hover-expand timer, and use_drag/use_drop wiring.
6 missed mutants + 2 timeouts from the initial mutation run (before the gate was removed) are deferred to Nightly CI triage — the killer tests added for them remain but the systematic gate is gone.
MAX_TREE_DEPTH intra-doc-link warning in a pre-existing struct doc is unresolved (not introduced by this PR, but noted as pre-existing lint noise).
The §6 rename-variant (Renamable Nodes) in tree-view.md was updated to use Key identity but the full variant implementation is out of scope for this task — deferred to a future task.
Lazy loading variant (§5) similarly has Key identity applied to the spec but the runtime lazy-fetch integration is adapter-level work, deferred to #366/#509.
